guide_review_response.work_item: v5 mid-stage handoff emission post-verification (Guide-#6 emission_protocol steps 1-5 retrospective audit)

reviewing.ea_artifact_committed: docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v5.xml at commit f863a22 (333 insertions, sole file in diff)

guide_review_response.summary:
EA-5's v5 mid-stage handoff is APPROVED. Independent disk verification confirms all 4 g6-ea5 acknowledgments are SATISFIED on disk: (n1) duplicate D4 comment block in server.py is deleted (single 44-line block at lines 13-57; module imports begin at line 60); (n2) D2 incompleteness is comprehensively encoded in v5 XML (work_completed_this_session §2.6_D2_PARTIAL marks status="PARTIAL_uncommitted_INCOMPLETE_PER_GUIDE_6" with full why_incomplete + guide_6_escalation citation + closure_path_for_ea_6 verbatim 6-caller list + BEFORE/AFTER pattern; work_remaining §2.6_D2_completion has priority="FIRST"; ack g5-4 in critical_carryovers/binding_acknowledgments tagged priority="CRITICAL_FORWARD_ACTIVE" with full verbatim text); (n3) v5 commit is docs-only (`git show --stat f863a22` shows ONE file in diff: EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v5.xml, 333 insertions); (n4) D4 decision retention acknowledged with forward-applicability clause. v5 XML schema fidelity to v1/v2/v3/v4 is excellent — all required sections present (metadata, ground_truth_at_handoff, work_completed_inherited, work_completed_this_session, work_remaining, standing_rules_carried_forward, critical_carryovers, successor_pickup_verification with V0a-V0l (V0l is a thoughtful new addition pre-empting count_delta confusion), successor_immediate_next_actions with 9 steps + if_budget_tight clause, handoff_metadata_for_audit with decision_log + files_touched_this_session_summary). Invariants preserved: HEAD advanced from 9277d61 → f863a22 with single docs-only commit; stash@{0} sdo 20260424_013002 byte-identical; state.json fleet_paused=true with last_updated_by="copilot_agent" + last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" preserved (no resume_fleet/pause_fleet cycle); pyproject.toml stays in M list per i1-3.
ONE substantive finding: the +1 M working-tree delta is MISATTRIBUTED in both Guide-#6's inter-deliverable review file AND EA-5's v5 XML count_delta_vs_guide_6_prediction block. Both attribute the +1 to docs/platform_separation/GUIDE_PROMPT.xml. The actual +1 is on tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py — which became M for the first time when EA-5 added the conditional load_config fallback (D2 partial) + new update_task public method (D3 enabler). GUIDE_PROMPT.xml has been M throughout the entire chain (v3 → v4 → 9277d61 → v5) and is not a delta. The substantive work is unaffected; this is purely a documentation accuracy issue in the audit chain. CORRECTION RECORDED in this verification file (which becomes the audit anchor); v5 XML and Guide-#6 inter-deliverable review file are immutable per no-amend doctrine but their misattribution is forward-corrected here.
THREE additional minor documentation defects in v5 XML (LOW severity; non-blocking): (a) git_log_oneline_5 block at line 60 has predecessor v4 hash as "(predecessor of 9277d61)" instead of the actual hash d0c6df1; (b) v4 commit message in same block is paraphrased rather than verbatim; (c) the v5 XML was authored before the v5 commit landed, so head_at_handoff_after_this_commit is descriptive ("this commit -- one parent ahead of 9277d61") rather than the actual hash f863a22. None of these affect EA-6's substantive work; all are forward-corrected in this verification file's <corrections_for_audit_chain> block.
Stage 2 single-commit discipline holds through v5 emission. EA-6 spawning is now appropriate. EA-6's first substantive action remains D2-completion 6-caller closure as documented in v5 XML work_remaining §2.6_D2_completion; no additional pre-EA-6 work is required from the LA or Guide.

independent_disk_verification.check: `git log --oneline -1` → f863a22 (HEAD) `git show --stat f863a22`: commit f863a22... Author Blair... Date 2026-04-26 message: "docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v5 -- D3 + D4 decision (DIVERGE) closed; D2 partial (load_config conditional but 6 callers still zero-arg, w15 escalation per g5-4); D1+2.7-2.10 deferred to successor" diff: docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v5.xml | 333 +++ 1 file changed, 333 insertions(+) ONE file in diff. Docs-only. Commit message matches Guide-#6 emission_protocol step 4 template. i1-4 + dn15 honored.

Read tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py lines 1-75: Lines 13-57: single 44-line decision block (DIVERGE rationale + 4-point analysis + disposition). Line 58: closing `"""` of the module docstring. Line 59: blank. Line 60: `from __future__ import annotations`. Line 61-71: standard imports + `from tools._vikunja_client import _require_project_id`. NO duplicate block. NO line-59 to line-103 second copy. Cleanup landed correctly. The Guide-#6 correction-1 is SATISFIED on disk.

Read v5 XML §work_completed_this_session §2.6_D2_PARTIAL (lines 129-154): - status="PARTIAL_uncommitted_INCOMPLETE_PER_GUIDE_6" ✓ - why_incomplete block: clearly explains 6 zero-arg callers still trigger conditional fallback ✓ - guide_6_escalation block: cites Guide-#6 review file + ack g5-4 verbatim ✓ - closure_path_for_ea_6 block: BEFORE/AFTER pattern verbatim, 6-caller list verbatim, pytest sanity check ✓
Read v5 XML §work_remaining §2.6_D2_completion (lines 170-179): - priority="FIRST" ✓ - do_this_first block: directs EA-6 to execute as first substantive action ✓ - success_criteria block: 4 criteria including grep-zero-hits + pytest-282 ✓ - ack_g5_4_satisfied_when block: clear transition rule from FORWARD-ACTIVE to SATISFIED ✓
Read v5 XML §critical_carryovers §binding_acknowledgments (lines 261-272): - g5-4 tagged priority="CRITICAL_FORWARD_ACTIVE" ✓ - forward_active_g5_4_full_text block (lines 274-282): verbatim ack g5-4 text + cite to Guide-#6 escalation ✓
Encoding is COMPREHENSIVE. EA-6 cannot miss D2-completion as the first action.

git stash list: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 — byte-identical. state.json: fleet_paused=true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" — preserved. pytest baseline (per EA-5 claim, not re-run): 282 passed. Credible: dup cleanup is docstring-only with no runtime effect; D2/D3/D4 substantive edits don't change call contracts in a way existing tests would catch (chokepoint guards still active; D3 named-method preserves request semantics; D2 conditional preserves zero-arg behavior).

All required sections present per v1/v2/v3/v4 pattern + thoughtful additions: - metadata block with predecessor/successor framing ✓ - ground_truth_at_handoff with full git status reproduction + count_at_v5_emission + count_delta_vs_guide_6_prediction (CONTAINS MISATTRIBUTION — see correction below) ✓ - work_completed_inherited (carry forward from v1/v2/v3/v4) ✓ - work_completed_this_session with explicit 2.6_D4 + 2.6_D3 + 2.6_D2_PARTIAL + 2.6_D4_dup_cleanup sub-items ✓ - work_remaining with 2.6_D2_completion FIRST + D1 + 2.7 + 2.7.v2 + 2.8 + 2.9 + 2.10 ✓ - standing_rules_carried_forward (v6 added to mid-stage handoff cadence) ✓ - critical_carryovers with full ack chain ✓ - successor_pickup_verification with V0a-V0l (V0l NEW; pre-empts count_delta confusion) ✓ - successor_immediate_next_actions with 9 steps + if_budget_tight clause ✓ - handoff_metadata_for_audit with decision_log (5 entries) + files_touched_this_session_summary ✓

EA-5 marked all 4 g6-ea5_to_emit acknowledgments as SATISFIED in v5 XML §critical_carryovers §binding_acknowledgments (lines 267-270): - g6-ea5_to_emit_n1 (D4 dup cleanup): SATISFIED — VERIFIED on disk (single 44-line block at lines 13-57; dup at 59-103 deleted). - g6-ea5_to_emit_n2 (D2 incompleteness encoding): SATISFIED — VERIFIED in v5 XML (comprehensive 5-block encoding across work_completed_this_session + work_remaining + critical_carryovers). - g6-ea5_to_emit_n3 (single docs-only commit): SATISFIED — VERIFIED via git show --stat (1 file, 333 insertions). - g6-ea5_to_emit_n4 (D4 decision retention): ACKNOWLEDGED — VERIFIED in v5 XML §work_completed_this_session §2.6_D4 with forward-applicability clause.

correction.topic: +1 M delta misattribution in BOTH Guide-#6 and EA-5 v5 XML
correction.guide_6_error: Guide-#6 inter-deliverable review file (2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_INTER_DELIVERABLE_REVIEW_EA_5_D2_INCOMPLETE_V5_AUTHORIZED.xml) <check id="invariants_preserved" verdict="PASS"> predicted "32 entries (21 M + 2 D + 9 ??)" and claimed "the 4 files EA-5 edited this session (server.py, legacy _vikunja_client.py, dashboard_maintainer.py, plus implicit re-saves) all already appear as M from prior 2.4 work and remain M." This was INCORRECT for tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py: per the v4 git_status_porcelain block (verbatim reproduction in EA-5 init pointer + v4 handoff XML), the legacy file was NOT M at v4 emission. EA-4 explicitly deferred it to 2.6 per g5-4 (file 12 disposition NO CHANGE in 2.4 closure), so it remained matching HEAD. EA-5's session edits (D2 conditional load_config + D3 update_task method) made it M for the first time. Correct prediction would have been 22 M + 2 D + 9 ?? = 33 entries.
correction.ea_5_error: EA-5 v5 XML §ground_truth_at_handoff §count_delta_vs_guide_6_prediction (lines 55-57) says: "Delta: +1 M on docs/platform_separation/GUIDE_PROMPT.xml (a docs-governance file owned by the LA; modified between Guide-#6's audit timestamp and v5 emission, OR Guide-#6's count omitted it because the file is considered governance not refactor). EA-5 has NOT touched GUIDE_PROMPT.xml in this session; the M state is inherited from prior cycles." This attribution is INCORRECT: GUIDE_PROMPT.xml has been M at every prior v3/v4 ground_truth snapshot AND at the LA's 9277d61 commit AND at v5 emission. It is NOT a delta. The actual +1 delta is on tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py from EA-5's session edits (which the v5 XML correctly identifies elsewhere in delta_vs_v4 lines 87-93 as "the 4 EA-5-edited files were ALREADY M from prior 2.4 work" — but this same statement was incorrect: the legacy file was NOT already M).
correction.correct_attribution: Independent disk verification at v5 emission timestamp: - 22 M files (21 from v4 unchanged + 1 NEW: tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py) - 2 D files (LA-side housekeeping deletions, unchanged from v4) - 9 ?? files (unchanged from v4 — temp_for_responses/ continues to grow with new files but the directory itself is the single ?? entry) - Total: 33 entries The +1 M delta vs v4 (NOT vs Guide-#6's prediction of 32, which was itself incorrect): tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py.
correction.impact_on_ea_6: EA-6's V0c verification will observe 22 M + 2 D + 9 ?? = 33 entries. EA-6 should cross-reference both v5 XML §count_delta_vs_guide_6_prediction (which misattributes to GUIDE_PROMPT.xml) AND this Guide-#6 verification file's correct_attribution block (which correctly identifies tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py). Functionally, EA-6 will discover the legacy file's M state when reading work_completed_this_session §2.6_D2_PARTIAL + §2.6_D3 (both correctly identify the legacy file as edited), so the misattribution does not block EA-6's work. It is a documentation accuracy issue affecting only the count_delta block.
correction.forward_correction: Both v5 XML and Guide-#6 inter-deliverable review file are immutable per no-amend doctrine. THIS verification file is the audit anchor for the correction. EA-6 init pointer (forthcoming) will reference both files + this correction explicitly so EA-6's iteration-1 comprehension gate can restate the correct attribution.

correction.topic: v4 commit hash placeholder in v5 XML git_log_oneline_5
correction.issue: v5 XML lines 58-64 git_log_oneline_5 block uses "(predecessor of 9277d61)" as the v4 commit identifier instead of the actual hash d0c6df1. EA-5 likely omitted it because the git log -3 output at v5 emission time only showed 3 entries deep (9277d61, d0c6df1, 9d17e7f) and EA-5 wanted to show 5 entries; rather than re-running git log -5 for accuracy, they used a parenthetical placeholder.
correction.forward_correction: EA-6 init pointer documents the actual v4 hash in its git history block; this Guide-#6 verification anchors the correction.

correction.topic: v4 commit message paraphrased in v5 XML git_log_oneline_5
correction.issue: v5 XML lines 60 paraphrases v4 commit message as "items 2.1+2.2+2.3+2.4 + f1 + 2.5 + 2.5.v2 complete (uncommitted), resume at 2.6". Actual v4 commit message: "Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v4 -- item 2.4 closed (all 13) + f1 fix + items 2.5 + 2.5.v2 complete (all uncommitted), resume at 2.6".
correction.correct_value: verbatim from `git log --oneline -3`: "d0c6df1 docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v4 -- item 2.4 closed (all 13) + f1 fix + items 2.5 + 2.5.v2 complete (all uncommitted), resume at 2.6"

correction.issue: v5 XML metadata line 10 has head_at_handoff_after_this_commit as "(this commit -- one parent ahead of 9277d61; the v5 handoff XML is the only file in the diff)" instead of the actual hash. This is because v5 XML was authored BEFORE the v5 commit itself landed (the XML cannot reference its own commit hash from within the same commit).
correction.forward_correction: EA-6 init pointer documents the actual v5 hash; this verification anchors the correction.

commendations_for_ea_5.commendation: EA-5 encoded the D2 incompleteness across FIVE separate v5 XML blocks (work_completed_this_session §2.6_D2_PARTIAL with 4 sub-blocks; work_remaining §2.6_D2_completion with 4 sub-blocks; critical_carryovers §binding_acknowledgments g5-4 entry; critical_carryovers §forward_active_g5_4_full_text; successor_pickup_verification §V0j + §V0k). EA-6 cannot miss D2-completion as the first action. This is exactly the "mature not minimal" encoding discipline the LA's mandate requires.

commendation.topic: V0l count_delta_acknowledged check is a thoughtful schema addition

commendations_for_ea_5.commendation: EA-5 added a NEW V0l verification check in successor_pickup_verification block that pre-empts EA-6 confusion about the 33-vs-32 count discrepancy. The check itself misnames the delta source (per correction C1) but the principle is excellent — anticipating the successor's possible source of confusion and providing an explicit acknowledgment slot.

All required sections present, with the same nested structure pattern. The handoff_metadata_for_audit decision_log has 5 entries documenting the substantive decisions of the session (D4 divergence rationale, D2 incompleteness framing, D4 dup cleanup, v5 emission timing, count_delta_acknowledged). Excellent audit-chain preservation.

stash@{0} timestamp 20260424_013002 has been byte-identical from v3 emission through v4 → Guide-#5→6 → v5 — four major handoff cycles. state.json invariants (last_updated_by, last_updated_utc) preserved verbatim through the same span. Zero resume_fleet/pause_fleet calls. This is the kind of discipline that makes Stage 2's single-commit-at-2.9 viable.

guide_review_response.next_action_for_la:
LA may now spawn EA-6 with the standard attachment set + v5 handoff XML + this Guide-#6 verification file + Guide-#6 inter-deliverable review file (which contains the v5 authorization) + the prior Guide-#6 EA-5 init pointer + the v4 quality review file + the 9 Guide-#5 disposition files + STATUS.md + cross-references. Guide-#6 will draft the EA-6 init pointer in the NEXT turn (per stop-after-gate discipline; no downstream artifact in this verification turn).
EA-6's first substantive action remains D2-completion 6-caller closure as documented in v5 XML work_remaining §2.6_D2_completion. The init pointer Guide-#6 will draft will encode all 4 corrections above + the count_delta correction + the BEFORE/AFTER 6-caller closure pattern + the standing instruction that EA-6 treat the v5 XML's count_delta_vs_guide_6_prediction block as misattributed (per C1) and trust this Guide-#6 verification file's correct_attribution block instead.
guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
APPROVED — v5 emission is verified clean on substantive work; documentation defects are forward-corrected here in the audit chain. EA-5 has retired. LA may now signal Guide-#6 to draft the EA-6 init pointer (next turn). After init pointer is drafted, LA spawns EA-6 with the attachment set; EA-6 runs V0a-V0l + emits iteration-1 comprehension gate; Guide-#6 reviews per ea_comprehension_gate_review procedure; EA-6 begins D2-completion 6-caller closure as first substantive action.
Standing reminders unchanged: single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only; mid-stage handoff XMLs (v1/v2/v3/v4/v5/+v6 if needed) are the EXEMPT docs-only commits; fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; STATUS append-only at item 2.10.
